_comment: Single source of truth for AI provider taxonomy. Edit this file to add/remove providers or models — both the BE (src/bootstrap/nodes.ts default-fallback) and FE (src/byok/lib/providers.ts wizard) read from here at boot. Pricing is per-1K-token to match the existing FE display format (multiply by 1000 for per-1M). All data verified against each provider's official docs in June 2026.

providers.label: Anthropic
providers.description: Claude 4 family. Strong reasoning, tool use, long context.
providers.apiKeyHelpText: Your key stays in the sample server's sqlite-backed credential store (AES-256-GCM at rest). Production deployments swap this for a managed KMS.
providers.customModelHelp: e.g. a current model id like `claude-opus-4-8` / `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`, or a beta release.
providers._capabilityNote: webSearch + audioInput deferred: Anthropic's web_search_20250305 tool requires custom stream-event handling (server_tool_use / web_search_tool_result blocks); audio input isn't yet in the public Messages API. Add when shipping Phase 4 v2.

providers.label: OpenAI
providers.description: GPT-5 family. 1M context window, broad ecosystem support.
providers.apiKeyHelpText: Your key stays in the sample server's sqlite-backed credential store (AES-256-GCM at rest). Production deployments swap this for a managed KMS.
providers.customModelHelp: e.g. `gpt-5.4-nano`, a fine-tune `ft:gpt-5.4-mini:org:name:id`, or a preview.
providers._capabilityNote: webSearch + audioInput deferred: OpenAI's web_search_preview tool requires the Responses API (different endpoint + body shape than Chat Completions); audio input requires gpt-4o-audio-preview (separate model lineup). Add when shipping Phase 4 v2.

providers.label: Google
providers.description: Gemini 2.5 family. Very long context, fast streaming, multimodal.
providers.apiKeyHelpText: Your key stays in the sample server's sqlite-backed credential store (AES-256-GCM at rest). Production deployments swap this for a managed KMS.
providers.customModelHelp: e.g. `gemini-3.1-pro-preview` or any model id from the Gemini model catalog. Note: gemini-2.0-flash is deprecated and sunsets 2026-06-01 — don't use it.
providers._notes: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is the recommended default (live-verified 2026-06-23: text + vision input). Flash-Lite avoids the reasoning-budget issue that can return empty completions when maxOutputTokens is exhausted by internal thinking. NOTE: the gemini-3.x cost values are APPROXIMATE (mirroring the equivalent 2.5 tier) until official preview pricing is published. dispatchGoogle only sets thinkingBudget:0 for `2.5-` ids, so a low maxOutputTokens on a full 3.x flash/pro model could still return empty — the KB media path uses 8192 so it's unaffected. Gemini 3.1 Pro (preview) added Jun 2026; its cost mirrors 2.5 Pro (APPROXIMATE until official preview pricing publishes), same convention as the 3.x flash tiers.

providers._comment: MiniMax — the real provider behind the `openwop-free` managed entry below. Hidden from the BYOK picker (`hidden: true`) because the steward pays for the managed-path usage; users shouldn't be able to pick MiniMax directly by name. Listed here so the backend has one canonical source for model capabilities + costs (no parallel-list drift between FE and BE). The dispatch adapter lives at backend/typescript/src/providers/dispatch.ts:463 and uses an OpenAI-compatible wire shape with native `<think>` reasoning blocks parsed via ThinkBlockSplitter.
providers.label: MiniMax
providers.description: OpenAI-compatible wire shape with native reasoning blocks. The Try-it-free path routes here.
providers.apiKeyHelpText: Stored server-side; not user-facing. The Try-it-free managed path supplies this from MINIMAX_API_KEY at boot.

models._comment: MiniMax-M3 is the latest model on platform.minimax.io (1M context, frontier multimodal coding) and is the managed runtime default (MINIMAX_MODEL in managedProvider.ts). `cost` intentionally omitted — MiniMax has not published public per-token pricing for M3 yet; add it here once official pricing lands. The system-message de-dup in nodes.ts is provider-agnostic (single system message is valid everywhere), so the M2.7-era HTTP-400 workaround holds for M3.
models.label: MiniMax M3

models._comment: MiniMax-M2.7 — prior managed default (205K context, confirmed pricing). Kept as a documented fallback: set MINIMAX_MODEL=MiniMax-M2.7 to pin it.
models.label: MiniMax M2.7

providers._comment: Managed provider — operator holds the API key server-side and pays the bill. Users sign in to use it without bringing their own key. The underlying provider + model are intentionally NOT exposed to the FE (no _internalProvider / _internalModel here); both are resolved server-side from MINIMAX_API_KEY + a default model id in the bootstrap step. The wizard skips the model picker and key-entry steps when `managed: true`. Daily token cap per signed-in tenant is enforced by the BE — see managedProvider.ts.
providers.label: Try it free
providers.description: No API key needed — sign in and start chatting.
